query: When FastAPI.setup() runs at app startup, what docs/OpenAPI endpoints does it wire up?
How does FastAPI avoid blocking the event loop when resolving a generator-based dependency?
How does FastAPI detect whether a dependency callable is an async generator, accounting for wrapped or partial functions?
What helper does form-body extraction rely on to check if a field is a valid bytes annotation?
What utility is used across the codebase to safely check subclass relationships without crashing on non-class types?
When you call include_router, what class is actually constructing the merged route table under the hood?
If APIRoute.matches changes its return type, what other route classes implement the same method and might break?
What class is APIRoute.matches actually a method of, structurally?
Where does FastAPI.openapi() get the routes' version info from when generating the schema?
What determines whether request body fields should be embedded as a single field vs a model, and what does that check depend on?
What other HTTP method decorators on FastAPI (besides get and post) share the same underlying default-value mechanism?
What field-extraction helper checks whether a parameter should be treated as a sequence field?
